---
title: "Customer Churn Prediction for E-Commerce"
description: "XGBoost, Random Forest and Logistic Regression to predict customer churn using RFM features on 286,000 e-commerce transactions"
author: "Rohith Krishna Thazhathethil Chandraprakash"
date: "2025-12-03"
categories: [Machine Learning, XGBoost, Python, Classification]
---
::: {.close-button-container}
<a href="../index.html" class="close-button" title="Close and return to Projects">
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
    <line x1="18" y1="6" x2="6" y2="18"></line>
    <line x1="6" y1="6" x2="18" y2="18"></line>
  </svg>
</a>
:::
## Overview

This group project builds a churn prediction pipeline for an e-commerce
marketplace. The goal is to identify customers likely to churn in October
so the CRM team can launch timely retention campaigns.

::: {.callout-note}
## Dataset
286,208 transactional records from September 2025, reduced to 279,621
after outlier removal at the 99th percentile.
:::

## Model Pipeline

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Raw Transactions] --> B[Data Cleaning]
  B --> C[Outlier Removal 99th pct]
  C --> D[Aggregation to Customer Level]
  D --> E[RFM Feature Engineering]
  E --> F[Train/Holdout Split 80/20]
  F --> G[Logistic Regression]
  F --> H[Random Forest]
  F --> I[XGBoost]
  G --> J[Model Comparison]
  H --> J
  I --> J
  J --> K[XGBoost Selected AUC 0.79]
```

## Key Results

::: {.callout-tip}
## Best Model — XGBoost
- **Holdout AUC:** 0.79
- **Recall:** 0.77 at default threshold
- **Predicted churn rate:** ~40% retained for maximum customer capture
:::

## Model Performance Comparison

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Model performance comparison across Recall, AUC, and Accuracy"

import plotly.graph_objects as go

models = ['Logistic Regression', 'Random Forest', 'XGBoost']
recall =    [0.65, 0.72, 0.76]
auc =       [0.76, 0.78, 0.79]
accuracy =  [0.72, 0.70, 0.68]
f1 =        [0.44, 0.45, 0.45]

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name='Recall',   x=models, y=recall,   marker_color='#4361EE'))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name='AUC',      x=models, y=auc,      marker_color='#7209B7'))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name='Accuracy', x=models, y=accuracy, marker_color='#F72585'))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name='F1 Score', x=models, y=f1,       marker_color='#4CC9F0'))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode='group',
    title='Model Performance Comparison',
    yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1], title='Score'),
    xaxis_title='Model',
    legend_title='Metric',
    template='plotly_white'
)
fig.show()
```

## Feature Importance (XGBoost)

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Top 14 features driving churn prediction by XGBoost gain score"

import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

features = ['orders', 'Less_Active', 'Days_LastPurchase', 'sales',
            'Avg_ticket', 'FMCG', 'Low_ticket', 'Home',
            'Perc_discount', 'FinServices', 'Care&Kids',
            'Fashion', 'Avg_num_items', 'Electronics']

importance = [261.09, 131.71, 36.79, 33.56,
              21.96, 19.37, 16.73, 16.18,
              15.35, 14.05, 12.98,
              12.98, 12.74, 12.36]

df = pd.DataFrame({'Feature': features, 'Importance (Gain)': importance})
df = df.sort_values('Importance (Gain)', ascending=True)

fig = px.bar(df, x='Importance (Gain)', y='Feature', orientation='h',
             title='Top 14 Features — XGBoost (Gain)',
             color='Importance (Gain)',
             color_continuous_scale='Viridis')
fig.update_layout(template='plotly_white', showlegend=False)
fig.show()
```

## Churn Rate by Business Unit

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Churn rate varies significantly across product categories, with FinServices standing out at 27.1%"

import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

data = {
    'Business Unit': ['FinServices', 'Electronics', 'Home',
                      'Care&Kids', 'Fashion', 'FMCG'],
    'Churn Rate (%)': [27.1, 10.3, 9.7, 9.2, 9.1, 6.7]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data).sort_values('Churn Rate (%)', ascending=True)

fig = px.bar(df, x='Churn Rate (%)', y='Business Unit', orientation='h',
             title='Churn Rate by Macro Business Unit',
             color='Churn Rate (%)',
             color_continuous_scale='Reds')
fig.update_layout(template='plotly_white')
fig.show()
```

## Key Findings

| Feature | Business Insight |
|---------|-----------------|
| Orders | Fewer orders = highest churn risk |
| Less Active | Customers already slowing down are most at risk |
| Days since last purchase | Long inactivity strongly correlates with churn |
| FinServices | 27.1% churn — card distribution, not core e-commerce |

## Recommendations

::: {.callout-important}
## Operational Strategy
Run XGBoost weekly. Flag top 40% risk customers. Send high-risk/high-value
segments personalised offers. Use low-cost nudges for high-risk/low-value.
Monitor recall weekly — retrain if AUC drops more than 5%.
:::

**Team:** Bolshakova Alina, Budhiraja Raunit, Lucio Pérez Wendy Monserrat,
Thazhathethil Chandraprakash Rohith Krishna, Yalamanchi Vyshnavi
